ops.title: cc-sudo Touch-ID sudo flow shipped (4 casks live); post-ship follow-ups done (B1 dropped, iOS token hardening merged, docs+housekeeping); only physical-hardware E2E taps remain

ops.body:
The full cc-sudo Touch-ID-gated sudo flow was implemented 2026-07-15/16 across four git worktrees under `/Users/yasyf/Code/wt-cc-sudo/` (plain git, jj NOT initialized) plus a new local repo `/Users/yasyf/Code/authkit`. Architecture is language-split (see [[cc-sudo-invoker-pin-boundary]] for the security model): **authkit** (new Swift repo, the signed SE/Touch-ID helper: ConsentGate/SEAttestor/CallerCheck/Vault/SECache + a thin CLI, exit codes 0/1/2/3/**4**), **synckit** (Go, gained `consent/` + `authkit/` packages + `synckitd consent` service/CLI, tags **v0.16.0** at merge — main is v0.15.1), **cookie-sync** (Go, migrated off its own keyhelper onto authkit + synckit consent), and **cc-sudo** itself (Swift: root-side `Verifier`, `run/exec/mcp/install/trust/uninstall/doctor`).

All four branches are GREEN and each phase went through a codex finder→refuter review with every accepted finding fixed and codex-verified: synckit `cc-sudo-consent` (17 commits), cookie-sync `authkit-migration` (6), cc-sudo `cc-sudo-impl` (7), authkit `main` (16 commits, **no git remote yet**). The frozen cross-repo contract lives in the plan at `/Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-14/plans/we-want-to-create-dazzling-naur.md` (amended: origin-host-bound subject `sha256(canonical(argv) ‖ 0x00 ‖ utf8(origin_host))`; no-silent-truncation display render rule; exit-code 4 = caller-rejected/usage vs 2 = no-auth-mechanism; binding-mismatch is fatal not retryable).

MERGES DONE (2026-07-16, all CI green on origin/main): **synckit** merged + **tagged v0.16.0** (cask released); **cookie-sync** cutover merged (release tag deferred — its cask depends on the authkit cask); **cc-sudo** merged with its AuthKit dep pinned to authkit `main` revision `6818dc5` (fixed a flaky parallel unix-socket test forward: dedicated thread per `OneShotServer`, not the global queue). **authkit** was re-created properly via `/repo-bootstrap` (swift layer, release OFF — it keeps the proven cookiesync-keyhelper-style `.app` cask pipeline, since the standard binary-cask release feature can't carry the SE/keychain entitlements), pushed to the new public `yasyf/authkit`, CI green; the 6 standard signing secrets are set from `op://OpenClaw/*`.

RELEASED (2026-07-16, all casks live on `yasyf/homebrew-tap`): **authkit v0.1.0** (signed/notarized `.app` cask), **cc-sudo v0.2.0** (Developer-ID binary cask), **cookiesync v0.15.0**, **synckit v0.16.0**. The provisioning-profile blocker was resolved: the `com.yasyf.authkit` App ID + a `Developer ID` provisioning profile were minted through the Apple portal via the agent-browser-with-cookies skill (key correction: keychain-access-groups is NOT a portal capability — it lives in the entitlements file; the bare App ID + Developer ID profile grants `SXKCTF23Q2.*` keychain access, covering the shared `com.yasyf.cookiesync.helper` group). The profile is at `op://OpenClaw/MACOS_PROVISION_PROFILE_AUTHKIT/credential` + set as a repo secret. Two release-infra fixes en route: authkit's release.yml runner was `macos-15` (Swift 6.1) → bumped to `macos-26` (Swift 6.2, matching CI); the failed first v0.1.0 tag was deleted + re-cut. cc-sudo now depends on authkit `from:"0.1.0"`.

POST-SHIP FOLLOW-UPS DONE (2026-07-17, this session — plan `we-want-to-create-dazzling-naur.md`):
- **E2E [AUTO]** green (`swift test`, 89 tests). The physical-hardware `[TAP]`/`[2-MACHINE]` acceptance is captured as a cc-notes doc (`cee09893`, "cc-sudo v0.2.0 E2E acceptance") — it productionizes the old spike RUNBOOK (now trashed): install-from-casks → `doctor` → `sudo cc-sudo doctor --probe-prompt` in Terminal AND over `ssh localhost` (settles the asuser-from-SSH question) → run approve/deny → MCP → live tamper → optional routed-peer negative. Still needs the user's admin password + Touch ID taps.
- **B1 (route cookiesync bare Safe-Storage reads through a new authkit `vault-read`) — DROPPED** (user, on a codex second opinion; cc-notes note `060deb10`). Keychain ACL trust is keyed to the accessing binary's code identity, so authkit (≠ `/usr/bin/security`) can't inherit the grant, and a prompt-free read on no-biometry Macs would hit `errSecInteractionNotAllowed` forever. `readSafeStorage` stays on `/usr/bin/security`; the biometry vault path already routes through authkit.
- **B2 iOS token hardening — SHIPPED & merged** (cc-present `ca5b634`, cc-runtime `3e7dd1d`, both CI incl. iOS-simulator green). Both `TokenStore`s (`Pairing.swift`) now write `kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly` (device-bound, no backup-migrate, no iCloud sync), migrate legacy items in place on read (monotonic: only upgrades backup-migratable classes, never loosens a device-bound one), and use atomic update-first + race-safe (`errSecDuplicateItem`/`errSecItemNotFound`-tolerant) + fail-loud keychain calls. Went through sol review + adversarial refuter. **No biometry variant** — both apps were traced to have NO attended token read (all reads are background/daemon; pairing writes, unpair deletes, no reveal UI), so per the plan's fallback it's baseline+migration only (an unwired biometry API would be dead code). Revisit only if a planned reveal/re-auth flow appears.
- **Docs**: cc-sudo README rewritten to the shipped `run/install/trust/uninstall/doctor/mcp` surface; cookiesync `docs/helper-signing.md` deleted (retired keyhelper pipeline). Both merged, CI green.
- **Housekeeping**: deprecated `cookiesync-keyhelper.rb` cask deleted from the tap; all integration worktrees under `/Users/yasyf/Code/wt-cc-sudo/` removed (dir gone) + merged local branches pruned. **Primary-checkout resync was SKIPPED** — cookie-sync's primary was dirty and cc-present's moved mid-session (concurrent sessions using them); every primary is already an ancestor of its `origin/main`, so nothing's lost — `git checkout main` them when idle.

REMAINING: only the physical-hardware E2E taps (cc-notes doc `cee09893`). The old cc-sudo skeleton (v0.1.0, only `hello`) is superseded.
